process_details: Heated to 5 °C above the melting point at 10 °C/min, held for 5 min, then quenched at uncontrolled rate to -50 °C and held for 15 min. Second heating extended to storage temperature.
detection_methods: DSC, MDSC, FT-IR imaging, polarized light microscopy
source_quote: terfenadine + paracetamol, tcryst mean 2.3 +- 0.6 days (PLM); storage T = 83 °C, Tginitial = 55.6 °C.
notes: Storage above Tg (T_s = Tg + 0.3*(Tm50:50 - Tg)). Crystallization detected by DSC after 5 weeks (melting endotherm at 132 °C). Phase separation detected by FT-IRi after 1 day.
review_reasons: DSC H/C/H cycle for GFA classification was NOT performed. Second heating ended at storage temperature, not above Tm. Therefore crystallizes_on_dsc_cooling and crystallizes_on_dsc_reheating set to null (measurement not performed for Class 1/2/3 assessment), and gfa_class_dsc should remain null. Pajula 2014 used DSC only for sample preparation, not for Baird et al. 2010 GFA classification.

Tg updated from Kilpelainen et al. 2020 (DOI: 10.1016/j.ejpb.2020.08.007), Table 2, DSC 10 K/min, n=3, replacing Pajula 2014 TMDSC value. Three pairs in Pajula 2014 (terfenadine-paracetamol, terfenadine-indomethacin, indomethacin-paracetamol) were re-measured by Kilpelainen 2020 with standard DSC 10 K/min, enabling cross-corpus consistency.

detection_methods: DSC, MDSC, FT-IR imaging, polarized light microscopy
source_quote: terfenadine + indomethacine, tcryst = nd (not detected) by polarized light microscopy; one of the mixtures studied, terfenadine-indomethacin, did not show any signs of crystallization during 21 weeks of storage. Storage T = 96 °C, Tginitial = 77.1 °C.
notes: Censored: 21 weeks (147 days) without crystallization detected by PLM. FT-IRi indicated two partly separated phases after 9 days.
review_reasons: DSC H/C/H cycle for GFA classification was NOT performed. Second heating ended at storage temperature, not above Tm. Therefore crystallizes_on_dsc_cooling and crystallizes_on_dsc_reheating set to null (measurement not performed for Class 1/2/3 assessment), and gfa_class_dsc should remain null. process_details: Heated to 5 °C above the melting point at 10 °C/min, held for 5 min, then quenched at uncontrolled rate to -50 °C and held for 15 min. Second heating extended to storage temperature.
detection_methods: DSC, MDSC, FT-IR imaging, polarized light microscopy
source_quote: terfenadine + ASA, tcryst mean 6.3 +- 3.1 days (PLM); storage T = 93 °C, Tginitial = 68.9 °C; Tg after 5 weeks = nd.
notes: Phase separation detected by FT-IRi after 1 day. Tg not detected after 5 weeks (likely due to crystallization).
review_reasons: Pair-Tg from Table 3 (TMDSC TOPEM, 2 K/min underlying), not standard DSC 10 K/min. Pure component Tg values in Table 2 are at 10 K/min but not used for pair-Tg field per schema. Pajula 2014 is the only paper in corpus with TMDSC-only pair-Tg measurement.

DSC H/C/H cycle for GFA classification was NOT performed. Second heating ended at storage temperature, not above Tm. Pajula 2014 used DSC only for sample preparation, not for Baird et al. 2010 GFA classification.

process_details: Heated to 5 °C above the melting point at 10 °C/min, held for 5 min, then quenched at uncontrolled rate to -50 °C and held for 15 min. Second heating extended to storage temperature.
detection_methods: DSC, MDSC, FT-IR imaging, polarized light microscopy
source_quote: indomethacine + paracetamol, tcryst mean 1.3 +- 0.6 days (PLM); storage T = 68 °C, Tginitial = 35.8 °C; Tg after 5 weeks = 25.2 °C, corresponding to Tg of pure paracetamol (24.3 °C).
notes: Crystallization detected by DSC after 2 weeks (melting endotherms at 93, 113, 125 °C). Tg in mixture after 5 weeks (25.2 °C) corresponded to pure paracetamol Tg (24.3 °C), indicating phase separation/crystallization.
review_reasons: Pair Tg after 5 weeks of storage (25.2 °C) is within 1 K of pure paracetamol Tg (24.3 °C), indicating phase separation/crystallization rather than a co-amorphous state at the 5-week timepoint (initial Tg = 35.8 °C is between pure components and acceptable).

DSC H/C/H cycle for GFA classification was NOT performed. Second heating ended at storage temperature, not above Tm. Therefore crystallizes_on_dsc_cooling and crystallizes_on_dsc_reheating set to null (measurement not performed for Class 1/2/3 assessment), and gfa_class_dsc should remain null. Pajula 2014 used DSC only for sample preparation, not for Baird et al. 2010 GFA classification.

Tg updated from Kilpelainen et al. 2020 (DOI: 10.1016/j.ejpb.2020.08.007), Table 2, DSC 10 K/min, n=3, replacing Pajula 2014 TMDSC value. process_details: Heated to 5 °C above the melting point at 10 °C/min, held for 5 min, then quenched at uncontrolled rate to -50 °C and held for 15 min. Second heating extended to storage temperature.
detection_methods: DSC, MDSC, FT-IR imaging, polarized light microscopy
source_quote: indomethacine + ASA, tcryst mean 0.3 +- 0.6 days (PLM); indomethacin-ASA crystallized in a few hours after preparation. Storage T = 58 °C, Tginitial = 26.5 °C.
notes: Crystallization within hours. Crystallization detected by DSC after 5 weeks (melting endotherm at 121 °C). Phase separation detected by FT-IRi after 1 day.
review_reasons: PLM induction_time SD (0.6 days) exceeds mean (0.3 days), indicating large uncertainty in detecting onset of crystallization (very fast crystallization within hours).

Pair-Tg from Table 3 (TMDSC TOPEM, 2 K/min underlying), not standard DSC 10 K/min. Pure component Tg values in Table 2 are at 10 K/min but not used for pair-Tg field per schema. Pajula 2014 is the only paper in corpus with TMDSC-only pair-Tg measurement.

source_quote: indomethacine + glibenclamide, tcryst mean 5.7 +- 2.3 days (PLM); storage T = 90 °C, Tginitial = 65.7 °C.
notes: Crystallization detected by DSC after 5 weeks (melting endotherm in Figure 10A). FT-IRi did not detect phase separation, possibly due to dispersed system with local 50:50 ratios.

source_quote: paracetamol + ASA, tcryst mean 2.7 +- 3.8 days (PLM); storage T = 41 °C, Tginitial = 5.1 °C.
notes: Predicted partly miscible (chi% > 0 = 42.4%); only mixture not predicted immiscible. DSC detected melting endotherm after 5 weeks. Phase separation detected by FT-IRi after 1 day.
review_reasons: PLM induction_time SD (3.8 days) exceeds mean (2.7 days), indicating large uncertainty in detecting onset of crystallization.
